compiled_state.title: Reducer strategy is dead code: no UUID dedup on replay (duplicate-UUID events double-apply)
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Summary
The reducer's strategy layer is dead code: reduce_ticket accepts/defaults LastTimestampWinsStrategy but never calls strategy.resolve, and replay performs NO UUID dedup — duplicate-UUID event files double-apply.

## Reproduction Steps
(verified on installed 0.4.0 == HEAD, temp store)
1. Create a ticket; rebar comment <id> "only-once".
2. Copy the COMMENT event file to a new filename (same uuid in payload, ts+1s).
3. rebar show <id> -> comments == ["only-once","only-once"].

## Expected vs Actual
- Expected (documented at ticket_reducer/_api.py:31-44): "dedup by UUID (first occurrence wins), sort by timestamp".
- Actual: _processors.py:347-416 replays raw files in FILENAME order, no UUID dedup (only snapshot-source-uuids skipped), sorted by filename timestamp not event timestamp. MostStatusEventsWinsStrategy equally unreachable. Verified: no .resolve( call site in ticket_reducer/.

## Impact
Merge-as-union convergence leans on UUID-level determinism; duplicate-UUID files (recovery cherry-picks, manual copies, fsck-recover paths) silently double-apply comments/edits/links.

## Source
- src/rebar/_engine/ticket_reducer/_api.py:197-244 (unused strategy param/default)
- src/rebar/_engine/ticket_reducer/_processors.py:347-416 (replay loop)

## Fix direction
Decide: (a) wire the strategy into replay (load -> strategy.resolve -> process), or (b) delete the dead strategy classes/param and add a minimal seen-UUID skip in replay_events, documenting filename-order replay. See validation notes comment for measured blast radius of each (incl. test_ticket_reducer_conflict.py interactions) before choosing.

## Suggested RED test
Duplicate-UUID event under a second filename -> applied exactly once; existing conflict-resolution tests stay green.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Duplicate-UUID events apply once (RED test first)
- [ ] Decision recorded: wire-strategy vs minimal-dedup + dead-code deletion
- [ ] tests/scripts/test_ticket_reducer_conflict.py and reducer suites green
- [ ] _api.py docstring matches the actual replay semantics afterward

comments.body:
IMPLEMENTATION NOTES + DECISION (experimentally validated in sandbox, 2026-06-09):
DECISION: minimal seen-UUID dedup in replay_events; do NOT wire the strategy. Measured reason: fully wiring LastTimestampWinsStrategy (sort by event timestamp) BREAKS the currently-passing tests/scripts/test_ticket_reducer.py::test_same_second_link_unlink_sort_order (dso-jwan): LINK uuid ffff... and UNLINK uuid aaaa... at identical timestamps rely on _sort.py's LINK=0/UNLINK=1 type-order rule, which a timestamp-only stable sort loses. Also the filename ts-segment is STRING-compared while the strategy sorts numerically (mixed-width legacy sec vs ns timestamps would reorder), and SNAPSHOT start_idx machinery is index-based over the file list — wiring a re-sorting strategy needs a replay-loop refactor. So: delete/deprecate the dead strategy param per the (b) path, keep filename-order replay, document it.
Validated diff (ticket_reducer/_processors.py, replay_events): declare seen_uuids: set[str] = set() next to valid_event_count; then AFTER the idx<start_idx skip and AFTER the snapshot_source_uuids skip:
    if event_uuid:
        if event_uuid in seen_uuids: continue
        seen_uuids.add(event_uuid)
(Placement matters: dedup only among events that actually replay -> zero interaction with compaction.)
Validated: dup COMMENT applies once (baseline 2); dup STATUS no self-fork; distinct-UUID multi-event unchanged; SNAPSHOT interaction correct (in-snap straggler still skipped; fresh post-snap dup pair applies once). Suites: test_ticket_reducer_conflict.py + test_ticket_reducer*.py -> 89 passed against the patch.
NOTE re MostStatusEventsWinsStrategy (dry-run question): it IS exercised directly by test_ticket_reducer_strategy.py/conflict tests calling .resolve — "unreachable" means specifically: never invoked via the public reduce_ticket replay path. Keep the classes' tests if classes are kept as documented utilities, or delete classes + their direct tests together under path (b) — record which in the fix commit.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: FINAL DECISION (closing the residual ambiguity): DELETE the strategy classes, the strategy parameter on reduce_ticket, AND tests/scripts/test_ticket_reducer_strategy.py together, in the fix commit, with the commit message recording this choice. The classes are not kept as documented utilities.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

verify_commands.dd_text: duplicate-UUID event applies once; conflict-resolution pins green
